title: quercus × duriensis
intro: Quercus × duriensis, also known as the hybrid of sessile oak (Quercus petraea) and pubescent oak (Quercus pubescens), is a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Quercus × duriensis include its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of this tree are attributed to its ability to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Quercus × duriensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in herbal remedies.
